steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The journalist claims UN population estimates "most likely included people outside the cities proper." To support this with table data, we need a data point showing a UN estimate that is meaningfully larger than the city proper population for the same city — the gap would indicate the UN count extended beyond city limits.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The UN estimated Bogotá's population to be 10,574,000, which is significantly larger than the reported population for the city proper.: This directly compares a UN estimate to a city proper figure for the same city and shows a large gap — exactly the evidence the journalist's claim requires. ✓
  • Choice B — The metropolitan area of Bangkok is significantly larger than 1,569 square kilometers.: This addresses land area, not population counts, so it cannot support a claim about how population was counted. ✗
  • Choice C — The reported city proper population of Bogotá is significantly larger than the reported city proper population of Houston.: This compares two city proper figures to each other but never involves a UN estimate, so it does nothing to show that UN counts extended beyond city limits. ✗
  • Choice D — All the city proper areas are below 3,000 square kilometers.: This is about geographic area, not population, so it is irrelevant to the journalist's population claim. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
